req.title: Data retention (audit)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-095 mandates three testable behaviours: (1) expired resources are purged and an AuditEvent is written (positive/EP), (2) resources exactly at the retention boundary are handled correctly (boundary/BVA), (3) a policy mismatch raises an incident rather than silently purging or silently retaining (negative/NEG). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'active' to 'purged' with audit trail intact (STC). A fifth test uses a decision table to validate that non-expired resources are never purged (DT). Together these cover all acceptance criteria stated in the requirement.

tests.name: test_expired_resources_purged_and_audit_event_emitted
preconditions: At least one resource exists whose retention expiry is in the past
Nightly retention job is invocable via POST /cron/retention
actions: Seed a resource with expiry date set to yesterday via <seed_expired_resource>
POST /cron/retention to trigger the job
GET the seeded resource to confirm deletion
Query AuditEvent store for an entry referencing the deleted resource
expected: GET on the purged resource returns HTTP 404 or 410
AuditEvent record exists with action=DELETE and resource reference matching the seeded resource id
trace: R-HLT-095: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_expired_resources_purged_and_audit_event_emitted(seed_expired_resource, authed_headers):
    resource_id = seed_expired_resource(days_ago=1)

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=authed_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code in (404, 410), f"Expected purge, got {get_resp.status_code}"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/fhir/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": resource_id, "action": "D"},
        headers=authed_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for purged resource"
    assert entries[0]["resource"]["action"] == "D"

tests.name: test_resource_at_exact_expiry_boundary_is_purged
preconditions: A resource can be seeded with expiry equal to the current date (today 00:00:00 UTC)
Nightly retention job is invocable via POST /cron/retention
actions: Seed a resource with expiry date set to today midnight UTC via <seed_expired_resource>
POST /cron/retention
GET the seeded resource
Query AuditEvent for the resource id
expected: Resource is purged (404 or 410) because expiry <= now
AuditEvent with action=DELETE exists for the boundary resource
trace: R-HLT-095: expired resources purged (boundary: expiry == today)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_resource_at_exact_expiry_boundary_is_purged(seed_expired_resource, authed_headers):
    # days_ago=0 means expiry == today midnight UTC
    resource_id = seed_expired_resource(days_ago=0)

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=authed_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code in (404, 410), (
        f"Boundary resource (expiry=today) should be purged, got {get_resp.status_code}"
    )

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/fhir/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": resource_id, "action": "D"},
        headers=authed_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1

tests.name: test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident_not_silent_purge
preconditions: A resource can be seeded whose retention policy label is unknown/invalid
Nightly retention job is invocable via POST /cron/retention
actions: Seed a resource with an invalid/unknown retention policy tag via <seed_policy_mismatch_resource>
POST /cron/retention
Check incident store or job response for an error entry
Verify the mismatched resource was NOT purged
expected: POST /cron/retention returns HTTP 200 (job ran) but body contains a non-empty incidents list
The incident references the mismatched resource id
The mismatched resource still exists (GET returns 200)
trace: R-HLT-095: incident raised on policy mismatch
R-HLT-095: expired resources purged (negative: invalid policy must not silently purge)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_policy_mismatch_raises_incident_not_silent_purge(
    seed_policy_mismatch_resource, authed_headers
):
    resource_id = seed_policy_mismatch_resource()

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=authed_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    body = resp.json()
    incidents = body.get("incidents", [])
    assert len(incidents) >= 1, "Expected at least one incident for policy mismatch"
    incident_refs = [inc.get("resourceId") for inc in incidents]
    assert resource_id in incident_refs, (
        f"Incident not raised for mismatched resource {resource_id}"
    )

    still_exists = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers
    )
    assert still_exists.status_code == 200, (
        "Policy-mismatch resource must NOT be silently purged"
    )

tests.name: test_resource_transitions_from_active_to_purged_with_audit_trail
preconditions: Resource lifecycle states: ACTIVE -> EXPIRED -> PURGED are supported
Nightly retention job is invocable via POST /cron/retention
actions: Seed resource in ACTIVE state with far-future expiry via <seed_active_resource>
Assert resource state is ACTIVE
Update resource expiry to yesterday via <expire_resource>
Assert resource state is EXPIRED
POST /cron/retention
Assert resource is PURGED (404/410) and AuditEvent chain covers all transitions
expected: Initial GET shows resource meta.state == ACTIVE
After expiry update GET shows meta.state == EXPIRED
After job POST /cron/retention resource returns 404 or 410
AuditEvent log contains entries for CREATE, UPDATE (expiry), and DELETE transitions
trace: R-HLT-095: full audit trail across state transitions
R-HLT-095: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_resource_transitions_from_active_to_purged_with_audit_trail(
    seed_active_resource, expire_resource, authed_headers
):
    resource_id = seed_active_resource()

    active_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers)
    assert active_resp.status_code == 200
    assert active_resp.json()["meta"]["state"] == "ACTIVE"

    expire_resource(resource_id, days_ago=1)

    expired_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers)
    assert expired_resp.status_code == 200
    assert expired_resp.json()["meta"]["state"] == "EXPIRED"

    job_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=authed_headers)
    assert job_resp.status_code == 200

    purged_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers)
    assert purged_resp.status_code in (404, 410)

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/fhir/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": resource_id},
        headers=authed_headers
    )
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])]
    for expected_action in ("C", "U", "D"):
        assert expected_action in actions, f"Missing AuditEvent action {expected_action}"

tests.name: test_non_expired_resource_is_never_purged
preconditions: A resource with a future expiry date exists
Nightly retention job is invocable via POST /cron/retention
actions: Seed a resource with expiry set to tomorrow via <seed_active_resource>
POST /cron/retention
GET the resource
Verify no DELETE AuditEvent was emitted for the resource
expected: No AuditEvent with action=DELETE exists for the resource
trace: R-HLT-095: only expired resources purged (decision table: expiry > now -> retain)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_non_expired_resource_is_never_purged(seed_active_resource, authed_headers):
    # days_ahead=1 means expiry is tomorrow — should NOT be purged
    resource_id = seed_active_resource(days_ahead=1)

    job_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=authed_headers)
    assert job_resp.status_code == 200

    still_alive = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/fhir/Resource/{resource_id}", headers=authed_headers
    )
    assert still_alive.status_code == 200, (
        f"Non-expired resource must not be purged, got {still_alive.status_code}"
    )

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/fhir/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": resource_id, "action": "D"},
        headers=authed_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 0, "No DELETE AuditEvent expected for a non-expired resource"
